title: Brain Tumour Surgery in Dubai: Types, Process and Recovery
metaDescription: Learn about brain tumour surgery in Dubai with Prof. Giv Sharifi. Discover expert insights on surgical types, recovery, and care for your neurological health.
excerpt: Explore the process of brain tumour surgery in Dubai, including surgical techniques, preparation, and recovery strategies guided by expert neurosurgeon Prof. Giv Sharifi.

content.text: Understanding Brain Tumour Surgery in Dubai

Receiving a diagnosis of a brain tumour can be an overwhelming experience for any patient and their family. When you are searching for brain tumour surgery in Dubai, it is essential to understand that modern neurosurgery has evolved significantly to prioritize both patient safety and the preservation of neurological function. Prof. Giv Sharifi utilizes advanced diagnostic tools and surgical techniques to address complex conditions with the highest level of precision. Whether the diagnosis involves a primary tumour or a secondary growth, the focus remains on creating a personalized treatment plan that aligns with your specific health needs and long-term recovery goals. By choosing a highly experienced neurosurgeon in Dubai, you ensure that your care is managed with expertise and compassion throughout every stage of the medical journey.

The process of managing brain tumours often begins with a thorough evaluation of brain tumour symptoms, which can vary widely depending on the size and location of the growth. Common signs may include persistent headaches, seizures, vision changes, or unexplained cognitive difficulties. Once these symptoms are identified, advanced imaging such as MRI or CT scans allows your medical team to map the tumour accurately. Prof. Giv Sharifi emphasizes the importance of early detection and detailed surgical planning to minimize risks. Understanding the nature of your condition is the first step toward effective brain cancer treatment and achieving the best possible quality of life after your surgical intervention.
Types of Surgical Procedures for Brain Tumours

There are several surgical approaches used to treat brain tumours, and the choice depends heavily on the specific characteristics of the mass. A craniotomy is one of the most common procedures, where a section of the skull is temporarily removed to provide the surgeon with direct access to the tumour. In many cases, neurosurgeons aim for gross total resection, which means removing as much of the tumour as possible without damaging critical surrounding brain tissue. Prof. Giv Sharifi frequently employs sophisticated navigation systems that act like a GPS for the brain, ensuring that the surgical path is as accurate and minimally invasive as possible for each individual patient.

Another specialized area involves glioma surgery, which requires a highly delicate approach due to the infiltrative nature of these specific tumours. Because gliomas often grow within functional areas of the brain, the surgical strategy must balance the extent of tumour removal with the preservation of essential neurological pathways. Prof. Giv Sharifi utilizes intraoperative monitoring and advanced imaging techniques to map these pathways in real-time during the procedure. By integrating these cutting-edge technologies, the surgical team can safely navigate complex anatomical structures, significantly reducing the likelihood of postoperative complications while maximizing the effectiveness of the tumour removal process for the patient.
Preparing for Your Surgical Journey

Preparation for brain tumour surgery in Dubai involves a comprehensive assessment of your overall physical and neurological health. Before the procedure, you will undergo a series of preoperative tests, including blood work, cardiac evaluations, and detailed neurological examinations to establish a baseline. Prof. Giv Sharifi will discuss the specific risks and benefits associated with your surgery, ensuring that you and your family are fully informed and comfortable with the proposed plan. This collaborative approach helps to alleviate anxiety and ensures that every aspect of your health is optimized before you enter the operating room, which is a vital component of successful surgical outcomes.

Patients are also encouraged to prepare their home environment for the recovery period following their discharge from the hospital. This might include arranging for assistance with daily tasks, ensuring that living spaces are safe and accessible, and planning for follow-up appointments with your neurosurgeon. Prof. Giv Sharifi provides detailed postoperative instructions tailored to your specific surgery, covering everything from medication management to activity restrictions. By taking these proactive steps, you can focus entirely on your healing process, knowing that you have a structured plan in place to support your recovery and long-term health after your neurosurgical procedure is completed.
Recovery and Rehabilitation After Surgery

The recovery phase following brain tumour surgery is a gradual process that requires patience and dedicated care. Immediately after the procedure, patients are monitored closely in a specialized intensive care unit to ensure neurological stability and manage any postoperative symptoms. As you transition to the general ward, the medical team will begin early mobilization and physical therapy to help you regain strength and function. Prof. Giv Sharifi works closely with a multidisciplinary team, including rehabilitation specialists, to create a personalized recovery program that addresses your unique needs, whether you require physical, occupational, or speech therapy to return to your daily activities.

Long-term recovery often involves ongoing follow-up care to monitor for any signs of recurrence and to manage the side effects of treatment. Regular imaging scans will be scheduled to track your progress and ensure that the surgical site remains healthy. Prof. Giv Sharifi remains committed to your long-term well-being, providing consistent support and guidance throughout your follow-up visits. It is important to remember that every patient’s recovery timeline is different, and staying positive while following your medical team’s advice is crucial. By maintaining consistent communication with your neurosurgeon in Dubai, you can navigate the recovery journey with confidence and receive the expert care you deserve.
Advanced Technologies in Neurosurgery

Modern neurosurgery has been revolutionized by the integration of advanced technologies that enhance surgical precision and patient safety. Prof. Giv Sharifi utilizes state-of-the-art equipment, including high-definition surgical microscopes and neuro-navigation systems, to visualize deep-seated tumours with incredible clarity. These tools allow for the identification of delicate blood vessels and nerve bundles that must be protected during the resection process. By leveraging these technological advancements, the surgical team can perform complex procedures with greater confidence, leading to improved outcomes and a reduced risk of neurological deficits for patients undergoing brain cancer treatment in the region.

Furthermore, the use of intraoperative monitoring has become a standard of care for many complex brain surgeries. This technology allows the surgical team to monitor brain function in real-time while the patient is under anesthesia, providing immediate feedback on the integrity of motor and sensory pathways. Prof. Giv Sharifi integrates these monitoring techniques into his practice to ensure that the brain remains protected throughout the entire duration of the surgery. This commitment to using the latest innovations in the field reflects a dedication to providing world-class neurosurgical care that meets the highest international standards for safety, effectiveness, and patient-centered outcomes in Dubai.

content.text: Frequently Asked Questions
What are the most common brain tumour symptoms?

Brain tumour symptoms vary significantly depending on the tumour's location, size, and rate of growth. Common indicators often include persistent or worsening headaches, which may be more severe in the morning, and unexplained seizures. Patients may also experience changes in vision, hearing, or speech, as well as difficulties with balance, coordination, or fine motor skills. Cognitive changes, such as memory loss, personality shifts, or confusion, are also frequently reported. If you experience any of these symptoms, it is important to consult a qualified neurosurgeon in Dubai for a professional evaluation and diagnostic imaging to determine the underlying cause.
How long is the recovery time after brain tumour surgery?

Recovery time after brain tumour surgery is highly individual and depends on the complexity of the procedure, the location of the tumour, and the patient's overall health status. Generally, patients spend a few days in the hospital for close monitoring before transitioning to home recovery. Initial healing of the incision site typically takes a few weeks, while a return to full daily activities may take several months. Prof. Giv Sharifi will provide a customized rehabilitation plan, which may include physical or occupational therapy, to support your recovery process and help you regain your strength and independence as safely as possible.
What is the success rate of brain tumour surgery?

Success in brain tumour surgery is defined by the safe removal of as much of the tumour as possible while preserving vital neurological functions and improving the patient’s quality of life. While outcomes depend on factors like tumour type, grade, and location, modern surgical techniques have significantly improved success rates. Prof. Giv Sharifi uses advanced navigation and monitoring tools to maximize precision during every procedure. It is important to discuss your specific prognosis and expectations with your surgeon, as they can provide a realistic assessment based on your unique medical history and the specific characteristics of your tumour.
Can all brain tumours be removed surgically?

Not all brain tumours can be completely removed, as some may be located in areas of the brain that are critical for vital functions or may be too diffuse to separate safely from healthy tissue. In such cases, the goal of surgery may be to perform a biopsy for diagnosis, reduce the size of the tumour to alleviate pressure, or remove as much as possible to assist other treatments like radiation or chemotherapy. Prof. Giv Sharifi will carefully evaluate your MRI scans and clinical presentation to determine the most effective and safe surgical strategy for your particular situation, prioritizing your long-term neurological health.
How do I choose the right neurosurgeon in Dubai?

Choosing the right neurosurgeon is a critical decision that impacts your treatment journey and recovery. Look for a surgeon with extensive experience in treating brain tumours, a track record of using advanced surgical technologies, and a commitment to patient-centered care. It is helpful to research their credentials, read about their specific areas of expertise, and feel comfortable with their communication style during your initial consultation. Prof. Giv Sharifi is dedicated to providing transparent, expert care for patients in Dubai and Tehran, ensuring you have the information and support needed to make informed decisions about your health and surgical options.
